originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'flags' is never read

presenceCondition:
(or (and (not CROSS_COMPILE) (not WIN32) (not NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES))
    (and CROSS_COMPILE WIN32 (not NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES))
    (and CROSS_COMPILE (not WIN32) (not NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES)))
model:
(define-fun WIN32 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CROSS_COMPILE () Bool
  true)
reasoning: c76df14dfb4.c L42: 'flags |= T_ESCAPE_SHELL_CMD' dead store inside #else (when !NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES). flags is assigned in each loop iteration but the value is discarded (reset to 0 at start of next iteration, never used outside loop). PC covers: (!CROSS AND !WIN AND !NEED), (CROSS AND WIN AND !NEED), (CROSS AND !WIN AND !NEED). These are the valid compilable configs where !NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES (note: !CROSS AND WIN implies NEED via auto-define, so that case is correctly excluded). New bug, correctly constrained PC.
